content:
- Completed story-22a task 01 and pushed commit `3a65629`.
- Added the runtime-owned runner metrics module, `/metrics` endpoint, webhook/reconcile timing plus failure/outcome metrics, and changed bounded reconcile apply failures to be durably recorded and retried instead of exiting the runner.
- Verified with `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`; also fixed the default verify-image long-lane contracts so the full workspace test lane is green.
- Left unrelated Ralph task-tree changes in the worktree unstaged and uncommitted: deleted old story task files plus new replacement task directories outside this task's scope.

- Read list-active-tasks skill and started task selection flow.
- Progress log read for agent Choose-Task; no prior notes were returned.
- Next: refresh task summary, inspect current_tasks/current_tasks_done/choose-task rules, resolve blockers, then write selected path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- Refreshed task summaries and inspected all active candidate task files.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks in current_tasks, metrics task 22a/02 is blocked by 22a/01 which is already done, and story 21 task 07 is explicitly suspended/out of scope for now.
- Next: select the highest-value unblocked failing task from the remaining stories, write it to .ralph/current_task.txt, append the decision rationale, and quit immediately.

- Selected story-22 structured JSON logging as the next task.
- Reason: no active failing bug tasks remain; story 21 retag workflow is explicitly suspended, story 22a/02 is blocked by an already-completed prerequisite and is a later follow-on metrics task, and story 22/01 is the earliest unblocked failing product-level task across the shipped images in this Rust workspace.
- Next after resume: execute .ralph/tasks/story-22-structured-json-logging/01-task-verify-all-images-support-structured-json-logging.md with TDD, then re-run make check/test/lint.

- Read prior progress log, current task pointer, and the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill files.
- Current task points to .ralph/tasks/story-22-structured-json-logging/01-task-verify-all-images-support-structured-json-logging.md.
- Next: read the task file, detect whether it ends in TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE, and follow the required branch immediately.

- Read the current story-22 structured-json-logging task and confirmed it had no TO BE VERIFIED / NOW EXECUTE marker, so this turn had to stay in planning mode.
- Used the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skills to inspect the actual shipped image surface and logging seams: runner main/lib, setup-sql main/lib, verify-service cmd/runtime, README, compose artifacts, and existing image/CLI contract tests.
- Determined the real boundary problem is inconsistent ownership of operator logs vs payloads across the three shipped images: runner and setup-sql use ad hoc println/eprintln, verify-service only partially uses zerolog, and setup-sql currently mixes payload/output concerns.
- Created the plan file at .ralph/tasks/story-22-structured-json-logging/01-task-verify-all-images-support-structured-json-logging_plans/2026-04-20-all-images-structured-json-logging-plan.md.
- Updated the task file to include a <plan> tag pointing at that plan.
- The plan ends with TO BE VERIFIED, as required.
- Next turn should review the plan, and if the interface still looks right, flip the plan ending from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE and then quit immediately before starting implementation.

- Re-read the active task and its existing plan, which was still marked TO BE VERIFIED.
- Sanity-checked the live entrypoints and CLI shapes in runner, setup-sql, and verify-service before promoting the plan.
- Confirmed the key interface assumption: setup-sql already uses `--format` for payload output, so structured operator logging needs a separate `--log-format` contract to keep stdout payloads and stderr logs cleanly separated.
- Next: flip the plan terminator from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE, append progress again, and quit immediately without starting implementation.

- Verified the plan against the actual command entrypoints and CLI types.
- Confirmed the plan’s interface choice is correct: `--log-format` should be the shared operator logging switch, separate from setup-sql’s existing payload `--format`.
- Updated .ralph/tasks/story-22-structured-json-logging/01-task-verify-all-images-support-structured-json-logging_plans/2026-04-20-all-images-structured-json-logging-plan.md so the marker is now `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Must quit immediately now. The next turn should read the task and plan, see `NOW EXECUTE`, load the tdd skill again, and start executing the red/green slices.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, task file, and the promoted structured-json-logging plan.
- Confirmed this turn is execution mode: the plan for story-22 structured JSON logging is marked NOW EXECUTE.
- Noted unrelated dirty Ralph/task-tree changes already in the worktree and will avoid reverting them.
- Next: inspect runner/setup-sql/verify entrypoints and contract tests, then execute the plan in red-green slices starting with runner validate-config JSON logging.

- Added a new shared Rust crate `crates/operator-log` with the shared `--log-format text|json` enum and JSON event writer used by Rust images.
- Runner now supports `--log-format`; `validate-config --log-format json` emits one structured stderr event with stable base keys while text mode preserves the old stdout summary.
- Setup-sql now supports `--log-format`; payloads stay on stdout while JSON operator logs go to stderr, and invalid config failures emit structured JSON error events.
- Verify-service now supports `--log-format`; validate-config JSON success is implemented through a zerolog-based command logger with stable base keys, and JSON command failures are wrapped so root does not print a duplicate plain-text error.
- Targeted green tests so far: runner validate-config JSON success; setup-sql JSON success + invalid-config failure; verify-service validate-config JSON success.
- Next: add verify-service JSON failure coverage, add runner/verify runtime JSON startup+failure coverage, add image-level contract tests for all shipped images, then run full make check/lint/test and update task metadata.

- Implemented the shared structured logging contract across all shipped images with `--log-format text|json`.
- Added shared Rust logging boundary in `crates/operator-log`, wired runner and setup-sql to it, and wired verify-service through a consistent zerolog command logger with duplicate plain-text error suppression for JSON mode.
- Added contract coverage for runner/setup-sql/verify validate-config paths, setup-sql invalid-config failure, runner runtime startup+failure, verify-service runtime startup+failure, and image-level JSON logging coverage for all three shipped images.
- Updated README and compose artifacts so the documented operator-facing activation path is the same tested `--log-format json` contract.
- Fixed Rust Dockerfiles to include the new `crates/operator-log` manifest in cargo-chef planner layers so image builds stay green.
- Final gates passed: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`.
- Next after quitting: run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, stage the task-related files plus Ralph bookkeeping, commit with the required `task finished ...` message including the green test evidence, push, then quit immediately.
